hypotheses: The state machine in TopModule transitions incorrectly when both bump_left and bump_right are asserted, causing walk_left and walk_right outputs to mismatch the reference model starting at time 120 [evidence: log_excerpt L9-L10 'Output ... has 83 mismatches. First mismatch occurred at time 120.']

The next_state logic in TopModule treats any bump (left or right) as a request to flip direction, which may not match the reference model's intended behavior [evidence: RTL L20-L21, L26-L27 'if (bump_left || bump_right) ...']

The asynchronous reset appears to function, but the output logic may not be correctly synchronized to the state transitions, leading to persistent mismatches after the first error [evidence: log_excerpt L9-L11, waveform_excerpt failure_time=120, testbench L59-L60]

The testbench randomizes bump_left and bump_right together, possibly creating illegal or undefined input combinations that the DUT does not handle as the reference does [evidence: testbench L69-L70, log_excerpt L9-L10]
likely_failure_points: TopModule next_state assignment in always @(*) block, specifically the handling of (bump_left || bump_right) [evidence: RTL L20-L21, L26-L27]
TopModule output assignment in always @(*) block, which may not reflect the correct state after simultaneous bumps [evidence: RTL L44-L59]
Testbench stimulus generation for bump_left and bump_right, which may be producing input patterns not handled equivalently by DUT and reference [evidence: testbench L69-L70]
State register update logic in TopModule, which may not be properly synchronized with output logic [evidence: RTL L36-L41]
recommended_probes: Add waveform probes for state, next_state, walk_left, walk_right, bump_left, bump_right, and areset in both DUT and reference modules to compare transitions at and after time 120
Log the values of bump_left and bump_right at every clock edge, especially at time 120, to determine the input pattern that triggers the first mismatch
Probe the reference model's state/output at the same times to see if the reference handles simultaneous bumps differently
Add assertions in the testbench to flag when both bump_left and bump_right are asserted, and compare DUT vs reference outputs in those cycles
analysis_notes: The failure is not due to reset, as the asynchronous reset is explicitly tested and no reset-specific warnings are present [evidence: log_excerpt L32-L34]. The first mismatch occurs at time 120 and persists for 83 samples, suggesting a persistent state/output divergence rather than a transient glitch [evidence: log_excerpt L9-L11]. The RTL next_state logic flips direction on any bump, which may not match the reference model's intent, especially when both bumps are asserted. The testbench randomizes both bump signals, possibly creating edge cases. There is only one attempt in the history, so no repeated signatures or stale patch strategies are present. Evidence is sufficient to focus debugging on the state transition and output logic, especially for simultaneous bump inputs.
